notes: Field report calls the straight dumbbell pathway North-South and gives approximate GPS coordinates confirmed with two GPS units. Cardinal wording is represented by the sector centerline with half-sector uncertainty.
Field report explicitly describes a straight pathway extending E/NE from the main circle. E/NE is encoded as 67.5 degrees true with 22.5-degree uncertainty; the report notes a slight curve.

Field report explicitly describes a separate straight pathway extending W/SW from the main circle. W/SW is encoded as 247.5 degrees true with 22.5-degree uncertainty; pathways were offset and slightly curved.

ICCRA describes the Celtic Cross formation as oriented to cardinal directions and identifies north and south outer circles. This is a documented structural axis; the catalogue diagram does not show a continuous connector.

ICCRA describes the Celtic Cross formation as oriented to cardinal directions and identifies east and west outer circles. This is a documented structural axis; the catalogue diagram does not show a continuous connector.
